customer.businessProblem: Junior associates spend 60-70% of their time extracting key clauses and summarising routine commercial risks in NDAs, MSAs, and supplier contracts. This is high-cost, low-value work that delays deal velocity and creates inconsistency in risk identification.
customer.desiredOutcome: Reduce first-pass contract review time by 50%. Improve consistency of risk summaries across the team. Preserve partner and associate approval on all client-facing outputs.

discovery.currentProcess: Associates receive contracts via email or document management system. They manually read each contract, extract defined terms, flag non-standard clauses, and write a summary memo. Senior associate or partner reviews and approves before sharing with client.
discovery.constraints: Legal professional privilege must be preserved. No client-identifiable data in AI training. All outputs require lawyer approval before client use. Firm has no IT team — external IT managed service. SRA compliance must be confirmed before deployment.
discovery.knownRisks: AI hallucination in legal context. Inconsistent clause language across counterparty paper. Privilege and confidentiality exposure. Junior staff over-relying on AI outputs. Partner resistance to AI involvement.
discovery.successDefinition: 50% reduction in first-pass review time on standard contracts. Risk summary consistency score above 80% across associates. Zero client communications sent without lawyer review.
discovery.buyerTeam: Managing Partner, Head of Commercial, IT Manager (external)

workflows.name: Contract Intake
workflows.description: Contract arrives via email or client portal. Assigned to associate in iManage.
painPoints: Contracts arrive in mixed formats (Word, PDF, scanned)

workflows.name: First-Pass Clause Extraction
workflows.description: Associate reads full contract and extracts key defined terms, unusual clauses, and risk indicators.
painPoints: Junior associates miss non-obvious risk clauses

workflows.name: Risk Summary Memo
workflows.description: Associate writes a summary memo identifying key risks, deviations from standard positions, and recommended negotiating points.
painPoints: Associates unsure of firm standard positions

workflows.name: Senior Review and Approval
workflows.description: Senior associate or partner reviews memo and approves or revises before client communication.
painPoints: Rework is common due to inconsistent associate output

systems.name: iManage Work
systems.notes: iManage Work 10 API available. Managed service provider needs to approve integration. Client matter data is privileged.

systems.name: Email (Microsoft 365)
systems.notes: Contracts often arrive via email attachment. Graph API available but scope of access needs to be defined carefully.

systems.name: Firm Standard Positions Library
systems.notes: Currently a Word document maintained by one senior associate. Not versioned. Needs to be converted to a structured format for AI ingestion.

dataSources.name: Contract Documents
openQuestions: What proportion are counterparty paper vs firm paper?
Are historical contracts available for testing?

dataSources.name: Firm Standard Positions
openQuestions: Is it complete for all contract types in scope?

risks.title: AI hallucination in legal clause extraction
risks.description: Model may misidentify, misquote, or fabricate clause content, creating legal risk if relied upon without review.
risks.mitigation: All AI outputs mandatory reviewed by qualified lawyer before use. UI must display source clause reference for every extraction. No AI output goes to client without approval.
risks.escalationTrigger: Any fabricated or materially incorrect clause extraction identified in testing or pilot

risks.title: Legal professional privilege — data exposure
risks.description: Client matter data processed by AI must not create privilege waiver or data leak risk.
risks.mitigation: Data processing agreement with AI vendor. Confirm no training on client data. SRA ethics guidance review. Counsel opinion on privilege position.
risks.escalationTrigger: Any client data leaving the firm's data boundary without authorisation

risks.title: Inconsistent clause language across counterparty paper
risks.description: Counterparty contracts use non-standard language that AI may not recognise as equivalent to standard clauses.
risks.mitigation: Curate training examples across common counterparty templates. Include uncertainty indicators in AI output. Flag low-confidence extractions for manual review.
risks.escalationTrigger: AI confidence below threshold on more than 20% of clauses in a contract

risks.title: Firm standard positions document is outdated and unstructured
risks.description: The Word document used as the AI's knowledge base for standard positions is poorly maintained and may produce incorrect guidance.
risks.mitigation: Document review and clean-up before ingestion. Assign ownership to Head of Commercial. Version control introduced.
risks.escalationTrigger: AI guidance contradicts current firm position on any standard clause

risks.title: Partner resistance to AI involvement
risks.description: Partners may be sceptical or resistant to AI in client-facing work, slowing adoption.
risks.mitigation: Managing Partner sponsorship and communication. Pilot with supportive associates first. Present results at partnership meeting.
risks.escalationTrigger: Partner formally objects to AI use on any client matter

pilotPlan.objective: Validate AI-assisted clause extraction and risk summary reduces first-pass review time by 50% without reducing quality.
pilotPlan.scope: NDAs and MSAs only. Standard commercial contracts in English. Excludes litigation, employment, and property matters.

successMetrics.name: First-pass review time
successMetrics.measurementMethod: Time logged per matter in iManage

successMetrics.name: Risk summary consistency
successMetrics.baseline: 65% agreement across associates on same contract
successMetrics.measurementMethod: Blind test — 5 contracts reviewed independently by 3 associates with and without AI

successMetrics.name: Lawyer approval rate
successMetrics.measurementMethod: Audit of all client communications sent during pilot

launchCriteria: SRA ethics guidance reviewed and confirmed
Data processing agreement signed with AI vendor
Privilege position confirmed by counsel opinion
Firm standard positions document reviewed and updated
rollbackCriteria: Client data accessed outside agreed boundary
Partner formally objects to AI use on client matter
pilotPlan.baselineMeasurement: 4 weeks of time-recording data from iManage reviewed for first-pass contract review tasks. Associate calibration exercise completed on 5 standard contracts.
pilotPlan.targetOutcome: 50% reduction in first-pass review time on NDAs and MSAs. 80% risk summary consistency. 100% lawyer approval maintained.
